key_insight: Higher reward-model quality does not necessarily improve small-model math reasoning: Oracle-refined traces can be worse than self-generated traces because they drift away from the student model’s native reasoning style.
engineering_relevance: For distillation pipelines, utility should be optimized with data compatibility and style alignment, not reward scores alone, to avoid harming SLM adaptation and downstream accuracy.
method: Compared Oracle-refined traces against SLM self-generated traces selected via rejection sampling.
Introduced Style-Aligned Refinement to keep the student’s native trajectory while preserving logical fixes from the Oracle.
Analyzed the tradeoff between logical repair and distributional drift as the cause of the paradox.
evidence_signal: Across Qwen2.5, LLaMA-3, and DeepSeek families, traces refined or synthesized by a stronger Oracle scored higher by reward models yet consistently underperformed self-generated, rejection-sampled traces.
watch_item: Style alignment may require careful tuning to preserve reasoning quality without reintroducing the distributional drift it aims to remove.
paper_title: The Quality-Utility Paradox: Why High-Reward Data Impairs Small Model Mathematical Reasoning
